title: Timer Implementation
summary: Implement 25-minute focus timer and 5-minute break timer with countdown display and core control actions.
businessValue: As a user, I want to run timed focus sessions and breaks so that I can apply the Pomodoro technique to manage my productivity.

stories.title: Display Focus Timer at 25:00
stories.userStory: As a user, I want to see a 25-minute timer display when the app loads in focus mode so that I know how much time I have for my focus session.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a user opens the app
acceptanceCriteria.when: the app initializes in focus mode
acceptanceCriteria.then: the timer display shows '25:00' in MM:SS format

acceptanceCriteria.given: the app is loaded in focus mode
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user views the main screen
acceptanceCriteria.then: the timer is displayed at 112px font size with 700 font weight

acceptanceCriteria.given: the user is in focus mode
acceptanceCriteria.then: the display continues to show '25:00'

definitionOfDone: Focus timer displays '25:00' on app load
Timer display uses correct font size (112px) and weight (700)
Timer display persists when timer is paused or idle
Component is tested with unit tests
Design matches UX spec color (#F8FAFC text on #1E293B background)

stories.title: Display Break Timer at 05:00
stories.userStory: As a user, I want to see a 5-minute timer display when I switch to break mode so that I know how much break time I have.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a user switches to break mode
acceptanceCriteria.then: the timer display shows '05:00' in MM:SS format

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Timer display updates on mode switch
acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer is displaying '25:00' in focus mode
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user switches to break mode
acceptanceCriteria.then: the timer immediately displays '05:00'

acceptanceCriteria.given: the app is in break mode
acceptanceCriteria.then: the display uses the same font size (112px) and weight (700) as focus timer

definitionOfDone: Break timer displays '05:00' when break mode is active
Timer updates immediately when mode changes to break
Timer display uses correct font size (112px) and weight (700)
Component is tested with unit tests
Design matches UX spec color (#F8FAFC text on #1E293B background)

stories.title: Start Timer Countdown
stories.userStory: As a user, I want to click a START button to begin the timer countdown so that I can begin my focus or break session.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Timer starts when START button is clicked
acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer is at '25:00' and not running
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user clicks the START button
acceptanceCriteria.then: the timer begins counting down toward 00:00

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Timer counts down in one-second intervals
acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer is running from '25:00'
acceptanceCriteria.then: the timer display updates to '24:59'

acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer has been running for 10 seconds from '25:00'
acceptanceCriteria.then: the timer displays '24:50' in MM:SS format

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: START button changes to PAUSE when running
acceptanceCriteria.given: the user clicks START and the timer is running
acceptanceCriteria.then: the button label changes to 'PAUSE'

definitionOfDone: START button triggers timer countdown mechanism
Timer counts down at 1-second intervals
Button label changes from START to PAUSE when timer is running
Timer display updates in real-time as it counts down
Unit tests verify countdown logic and interval timing
Integration tests verify button click triggers countdown
Timer state is managed in component or service layer

stories.title: Pause Running Timer
stories.userStory: As a user, I want to click a PAUSE button to stop the timer countdown so that I can temporarily suspend my focus or break session.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Timer stops when PAUSE is clicked
acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer is running at '23:45'
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user clicks the PAUSE button
acceptanceCriteria.then: the timer stops counting down and remains at '23:45'

acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer is paused
acceptanceCriteria.then: the button label changes back to 'START'

acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer is paused at '23:45'
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user clicks the START button
acceptanceCriteria.then: the timer resumes counting down from '23:45'

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Timer time is preserved on pause
acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer is running
acceptanceCriteria.then: the remaining time is preserved exactly as it was at pause time

definitionOfDone: PAUSE button stops the timer countdown
Timer display preserves remaining time when paused
Button label changes from PAUSE to START when timer is paused
Paused timer can be resumed from where it was paused
Unit tests verify pause logic and time preservation
Integration tests verify button click pauses countdown

stories.title: Reset Timer to Default Duration
stories.userStory: As a user, I want to click a RESET button to return the timer to its default duration so that I can quickly restart a session without waiting.

acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer is running or paused at any value in focus mode
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user clicks the RESET button
acceptanceCriteria.then: the timer returns to '25:00' and stops counting down

acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer is running or paused at any value in break mode
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user clicks the RESET button
acceptanceCriteria.then: the timer returns to '05:00' and stops counting down

acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer is actively running
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user clicks the RESET button
acceptanceCriteria.then: the timer stops counting and displays the default duration

acceptanceCriteria.given: the user views the RESET button
acceptanceCriteria.then: the RESET button is secondary in styling with transparent background and border

definitionOfDone: RESET button stops the running timer
RESET restores focus timer to '25:00'
RESET restores break timer to '05:00'
RESET respects current timer mode when resetting
Button styling matches UX spec (transparent background, #475569 border)
Unit tests verify reset logic for both modes
Integration tests verify button click resets timer

stories.title: Display Timer in MM:SS Format
stories.userStory: As a user, I want the timer to always display in MM:SS format so that I can quickly read the remaining time.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Timer displays with leading zero in minutes
acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer has less than 10 minutes remaining
acceptanceCriteria.then: the timer displays with leading zero (e.g., '05:30', '09:15')

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Timer displays with leading zero in seconds
acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer has less than 10 seconds remaining in a minute
acceptanceCriteria.then: the timer displays with leading zero (e.g., '15:05', '24:03')

acceptanceCriteria.given: the app is in focus mode
acceptanceCriteria.then: the timer displays '25:00' not '25:0' or '250'

acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer is counting down from '25:00'
acceptanceCriteria.when: the timer reaches various time points
acceptanceCriteria.then: format remains consistent (e.g., '00:03', '00:01', never '0:3' or ':3')

definitionOfDone: Timer always displays as MM:SS with leading zeros
Formatting is applied to all timer displays (focus, break, countdown)
Edge cases tested (single digit minutes/seconds)
Format validation in unit tests
No formatting regressions in countdown display
